questions.prompt: Some people believe that students should be required to take a personal finance course before graduating from high school or beginning college. They argue that budgeting, saving, credit, and loan decisions affect nearly every adult. Others believe that schools already have many required subjects and that personal finance can be learned through family experience, work, or independent research. Assignment: Write an essay of 300–600 words in which you develop your point of view on the issue presented in the passage. Support your position with reasoning and examples from your reading, studies, experience, or observations.
questions.modelAnswer: A strong response should state a clear position on requiring a personal finance course, support that position with specific reasons and examples, address the issue thoughtfully, and demonstrate organized, controlled writing.
questions.scoringGuidance: A strong response should state a clear position on requiring a personal finance course, support that position with specific reasons and examples, address the issue thoughtfully, and demonstrate organized, controlled writing.
questions.rubric: Scores range from 8 for a focused, well-developed, logically organized essay with strong language control to 0 for a blank, off-topic, too short, incoherent, or non-English response.
